blocks.text: Camshaft removal and inspection
1. Crankshaft Pulley TDC Mark Alignment
Rotate the crankshaft pulley clockwise to align the alignment mark (A) with the TDC mark (white marking) (B).

items: Confirmation shall be made from the specified viewing direction (C).

blocks.text: 2. Cam chain tensioner removal
3. Cylinder head cover removal
4. Camshaft removal
Loosen the camshaft holders several times in numerical order.
Remove the camshaft holder (A) and camshaft (B).

items: The cam chain should be suspended by a wire or other means to prevent it from being dropped into the case.

blocks.text: 5. VTC actuator, exhaust camshaft sprocket removal
Cover the VTC slow angle hole (A) on the intake camshaft with vinyl tape and secure with tie wrap.
Seal one of the VTC advance holes (A) on the intake camshaft with vinyl tape.
Apply air through the advance hole (A) not blocked by the vinyl tape to release the lock pin.
Protect the camshaft with a rag and fix it with a vise. Remove the VTC actuator (A).

items: Do not damage the camshaft.

blocks.text: Remove the exhaust camshaft sprocket (A).
1. Rocker arm/hydraulic tappet removal
Remove the rocker arm (A) and hydraulic tappet (B).
2. Camshaft inspection

With the rocker arm and hydraulic tappet removed, install the camshaft and camshaft holder, apply engine oil to the threads and seat of the camshaft holder bolts, and tighten them several times in numerical order. Specified torque － : 8.0N·m (0.82kgf·m)
Inspect the camshaft for axial misalignment. Camshaft axial misalignment Std. 0.075－0.175mm Limit value: 1 0.375mm

items: The measurement shall be made without scratching or dents on the gasket mating surface of the cylinder head.
Inspect with the camshaft in the top dead center position (knock pin up) of the No. 1 piston.
Set the dial gauge and move the camshaft axially to read the dial gauge.
If the limit value is exceeded, replace the camshaft and re-inspect. If the limit value is still exceeded during the re-inspection, replace the cylinder head.

items.text: The measurement shall be made without scratching or dents on the gasket mating surface of the cylinder head.
The measurement shall be made without scratching or dents on the gasket mating surface of the cylinder head.
Inspect with the camshaft in the top dead center position (knock pin up) of the No. 1 piston.
Inspect with the camshaft in the top dead center position (knock pin up) of the No. 1 piston.
Set the dial gauge and move the camshaft axially to read the dial gauge.

If the limit value is exceeded, replace the camshaft and re-inspect. If the limit value is still exceeded during the re-inspection, replace the cylinder head.

blocks.text: Loosen and remove the camshaft holders in the order of their number. Remove and clean the camshaft and inspect the cam and journal sections for damage. Clean the bearing portion of the camshaft holder and set the camshaft in the cylinder head. Cut a plastigauge to the axial length of each journal and place it on the camshaft journal.

items: Do not rotate the camshaft during inspection.

blocks.text: Install the camshafts and camshaft holders, lubricate the threads and seat surfaces of the camshaft holder bolts with engine oil, and tighten the bolts several times in numerical order. Specified torque － : 8.0N·m (0.82kgf·m)

Remove the camshaft holder and measure the crushed width of the plastigauge with the scale printed on the bag of the plastigauge. Camshaft oil clearance No. 1 Journal:. Std. 0.030－0.069mm Limit value: 1 0.12mm No. 2, 3, 4 Journal:. Std. 0.060－0.090mm Limit value: 1 0.15mm

items: Measurements should be taken at the widest crushed point.
If the limit value is exceeded, replace the cylinder head and re-inspect. If the limit value is still exceeded during the re-inspection, replace the camshafts.

items.text: Measurements should be taken at the widest crushed point.
Measurements should be taken at the widest crushed point.
If the limit value is exceeded, replace the cylinder head and re-inspect. If the limit value is still exceeded during the re-inspection, replace the camshafts.
If the limit value is exceeded, replace the cylinder head and re-inspect. If the limit value is still exceeded during the re-inspection, replace the camshafts.

blocks.text: Measure the runout of the camshaft. Camshaft runout Std. 0.03 mm or less

items: The journal sections at both ends shall be supported by V-blocks or other means.
Inspect camshaft runout with a dial gauge.
Inspect while rotating the camshaft.
If runout is greater than the standard value, the camshaft should be replaced.

items.text: The journal sections at both ends shall be supported by V-blocks or other means.
The journal sections at both ends shall be supported by V-blocks or other means.
Inspect camshaft runout with a dial gauge.
Inspect camshaft runout with a dial gauge.
Inspect while rotating the camshaft.
Inspect while rotating the camshaft.
If runout is greater than the standard value, the camshaft should be replaced.
If runout is greater than the standard value, the camshaft should be replaced.

blocks.text: Inspect cam surfaces for damage or wear and measure height (A). Cam Height Intake: 1 Std. 33.263mm Exhaust: (1) Std. 33.260mm
1. Hydraulic tappet Air bleed
2. Rocker arm/hydraulic tappet mounting
Install the rocker arm (A) and hydraulic tappet (B).

items: The rocker arm must be mounted in such a way that it does not tilt.

blocks.text: 3. VTC actuator/exhaust camshaft sprocket installation

Protect the camshaft with a rag and fix it with a vise. Apply engine oil to the threads of the VTC actuator mounting bolts and the exhaust camshaft sprocket mounting bolts, and to the washer seat surfaces. Install the exhaust camshaft sprocket (A).

items: Do not damage the camshaft.
The camshaft locating pin (B) and camshaft locating groove (C) shall be aligned.

blocks.text: Install the VTC actuator (A).

items: Align the camshaft locating pin (B) with the VTC actuator locating groove (C).

blocks.text: 4. Crankshaft Pulley TDC Mark Alignment
Align the crankshaft pulley and align the mark (A) with the TDC mark (white stamping) (B).

items: Confirmation shall be made from the specified viewing direction (C).

blocks.text: 5. Camshaft Mounting
Install camshaft holder and camshaft.

items: The camshaft holder must be assembled with the arrow pointing toward the cam chain.
Check to make sure that the rocker arm is not tilted after the camshaft is installed.

items.text: The camshaft holder must be assembled with the arrow pointing toward the cam chain.
The camshaft holder must be assembled with the arrow pointing toward the cam chain.
Check to make sure that the rocker arm is not tilted after the camshaft is installed.
Check to make sure that the rocker arm is not tilted after the camshaft is installed.

blocks.text: Lubricate the threads and seat surfaces of the camshaft holder bolts with engine oil and tighten them several times in numerical order. Specified torque － : 8.0N·m (0.82kgf·m) Fix the hexagonal part of the camshaft with a wrench, etc., and turn the VTC actuator clockwise to lock it.

With the punch marks (A) of the VTC actuator and exhaust camshaft sprocket visible, turn the TDC marks (B) outward and align them with the upper edge of the cylinder head. Engage the cam chain in the following order: exhaust camshaft sprocket, VTC actuator. When installing the cam chain without removing the chain case, it is not necessary to align the punch marks on the VTC actuator and exhaust camshaft sprocket with the collar plate on the cam chain.

items: No slack in the cam chain between the exhaust camshaft sprocket and crankshaft sprocket, and between the VTC actuator and the exhaust camshaft sprocket.

blocks.text: 6. Cam Chain Auto Tensioner Installation
7. Valve timing Check
Turn the crankshaft clockwise two turns to align the alignment mark (A) with the TDC mark (white marking) (B).

items: Confirmation shall be made from the specified viewing direction (C).

blocks.text: Check that the TDC mark (A) on the VTC actuator and exhaust camshaft sprocket is aligned with the upper end face of the cylinder head.
8. Cylinder Head Cover Installation
